name: Pola de Allande and Church of Santa María de Zalón Loop
name_original: Pola de Allande a Iglesia de Santa María de Zalón — circular por Carretera Llinares
description: This difficult 12.0-mile (19.4 km) touring cycling route from Pola de Allande features 1896 feet of elevation gain through Asturian mountain
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Allande traverse a mountainous landscape in Asturias, Spain, characterized by varied inclines and descents. The region features rugged terrain and serene environments, providing a backdrop for cyclists seeking both challenging routes and scenic rides. Allande is also part of the Primitive Way of St. James, offering historic pilgrimage routes through diverse natural features. The area's natural environment includes a mix of forested hills and valley paths. Best touring cycling routes around Allande The most popular touring cycling route is Cangas del Narcea - Puerto de Leitariegos Loop, a 60.8 miles (97.9 km) trail that takes 6 hours 56 minutes to complete. This route offers significant elevation gain through mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Courias to El Puelu Trail, a difficult 11.4 miles (18.3 km) path. It leads through forested areas with notable elevation changes. Local touring cyclists also love the Santuario del Acebo Loop from Cangas del Narcea, a 17.2 miles (27.7 km) trail leading through mountainous landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 21 minutes. Touring cycling around Allande is defined by mountainous landscapes, forested valleys, and historic pilgrimage routes. The network offers options for both easy and more challenging ability levels. The routes in Allande are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 7 reviews. More than 200 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Allande's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when touring cycling in Allande?
A: Allande is characterized by a mountainous landscape with varied inclines and descents, providing a mix of challenging routes and scenic rides. You'll encounter rugged terrain, forested areas, and valley paths, with many routes featuring significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Allande?
A: While most touring cycling routes in Allande are rated as difficult due to the mountainous terrain, there is at least one easy route available. It's advisable to check the specific route details on komoot to match your skill level and preferences.

Q: What historical or cultural landmarks can I see along touring cycling routes in Allande?
A: Allande is rich in history and culture. You can explore the 'Indiano' architecture in Pola de Allande, visit the majestic 15th-century Cienfuegos Palace, or discover the ancient Iron Age settlement of Castro de San Chuis. The region also features the 12th-century Romanic Church of Celon, known for its medieval paintings. Many routes also follow sections of the historic Primitive Way of St. James.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Allande?
A: Yes, Allande offers several circular touring cycling routes. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1370425610/cangas-del-narcea-puerto-de-leitariegos-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cangas del Narcea - Puerto de Leitariegos Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is a longer, challenging ride. Another popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1369859824/santuario-del-acebo-loop-from-cangas-del-narcea" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santuario del Acebo Loop from Cangas del Narcea&lt;/a&gt;, offering a mountainous experience.

Q: What natural features can be observed while cycling in Allande?
A: Beyond the mountainous relief, Allande's natural environment includes forested hills and scenic valley paths. The region is also recognized as a 'Starlight Council,' meaning it offers exceptional conditions for stargazing, particularly along its 'Star Route' on the Primitive Way. You'll also encounter various mountain passes and peaks, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2873224" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de La Marta&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6493683" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto del Palo Pass&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any specific routes that pass through forested areas?
A: Yes, some routes lead through beautiful forested areas. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1368853654/courias-to-el-puelu-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Courias to El Puelu Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult path that takes you through notable forested sections with significant elevation changes.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Allande?
A: Given Allande's mountainous terrain, the spring and autumn months generally offer the most pleasant conditions for touring cycling, with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can also be suitable, but be prepared for potentially warmer weather, especially on exposed climbs. Winter cycling may be challenging due to colder temperatures and possible snow at higher elevations.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about their experience in Allande?
A: The touring cycling routes in Allande are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 7 reviews. Cyclists often praise the challenging mountainous landscapes, the serene environment, and the opportunity to explore historic pilgrimage routes like the Primitive Way of St. James.

Q: Are there any long-distance touring cycling options in Allande?
A: Yes, for those seeking longer distances, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1370425610/cangas-del-narcea-puerto-de-leitariegos-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cangas del Narcea - Puerto de Leitariegos Loop&lt;/a&gt; covers nearly 100 km (60.8 miles) and involves significant elevation gain, making it a substantial challenge for experienced touring cyclists.

Q: Can I find routes that are part of the Primitive Way of St. James in Allande?
A: Absolutely. Allande is a significant stage on the Primitive Way of St. James. Many touring cycling routes in the region incorporate sections of this historic pilgrimage path, offering a unique blend of natural beauty and cultural heritage. The local council has even adapted disused tracks to enhance the cycling experience on this route.

Q: Are there any specific points of interest related to mountain passes or peaks?
A: Allande is home to several notable mountain passes and peaks. You can find highlights such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2873224" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de La Marta&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5593878" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo de las Mujeres Muertas Pass&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1139780" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Highest Point of the Camino Primitivo&lt;/a&gt;. These offer stunning views and challenging climbs for touring cyclists.

Q: What is the overall difficulty level of touring cycling in Allande?
A: The majority of touring cycling routes in Allande are rated as difficult, with 30 out of 31 available tours falling into this category. This is primarily due to the region's mountainous terrain and the significant elevation changes encountered on most paths. Cyclists should be prepared for challenging climbs and descents.
tips: Allande, which borders Galicia, is one of the least known towns in Asturias. It is one of the few towns where Eonaviego is still spoken, also known as Galician-Asturian.
This beautiful mural lets you know that you are on the right path to Santiago. It also welcomes you to the small town of Pola de Allande.
Here you will find a very simple but wonderful Romanesque style church. It is worth stopping and entering if possible to see the occasional old painting on the walls.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: Komoot estimates the cycling time for this circular route to be around 1 hour and 34 minutes. However, given its 'difficult' rating and significant elevation changes, actual completion time can vary based on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the views.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is officially categorized as 'difficult' and requires 'very good fitness'. With a substantial elevation gain of 578 meters over 19.4 kilometers, it presents challenging climbs and exhilarating descents, making it unsuitable for beginner cyclists.

faqs.question: Where does the route start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Pola de Allande. As a well-known base for cycling enthusiasts, Pola de Allande offers amenities including hotels and restaurants, suggesting that parking facilities are available within the town for visitors.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily consists of paved surfaces, characteristic of hard road rides in the region. However, be prepared for challenging climbs and exhilarating descents through mountainous landscapes. While mostly paved, some segments in the broader area can be unpaved, so be ready for potentially mixed surfaces.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery will I experience along the way?
faqs.answer: You can expect breathtaking mountain landscapes typical of Asturias. The area is known for its green and lush mountains, and you might see beautiful yellow flowers blooming. Ascents offer stunning views of the valley, the River Nison, and the Lugo Mountains, showcasing Asturias as a 'natural paradise' with spectacular mountains and peaceful forests.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is generally during the spring, summer, and early autumn months. These seasons offer the most favorable weather conditions, avoiding the potential for snow or extreme cold in the mountainous terrain, and allowing you to fully enjoy the lush landscapes.

faqs.question: What landmarks or points of interest will I see on this route?
faqs.answer: Along this route, you'll encounter several notable points of interest. These include the starting point, Pola de Allande, the Mural of the Camino de Santiago, and the Church of Santa María de Zalón, which is a key destination on the circular journey.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with sections of several other trails. Notably, it includes parts of the historic Camino Primitivo, as well as segments of Etapa 4: Tinéu - Pola de Allande, Molín de Valbona, and Etapa 5: Pola de Allande - A Mesa.

faqs.question: Is Pola de Allande to Iglesia de Santa María de Zalón — circular by Carretera Llinares dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is a cycling route primarily on roads, the general area around Pola de Allande is welcoming to outdoor activities. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure they are well-behaved and can keep pace with a bicycle, or consider alternative walking trails in the region that are explicitly dog-friendly.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route. It utilizes public roads and trails in the region, making it freely accessible to cyclists.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or accommodation along the route?
faqs.answer: The starting and ending point, Pola de Allande, is described as an excellent base with amenities such as hotels, restaurants, and even a swimming pool. While the route itself is primarily through mountainous terrain, you can find all necessary services in Pola de Allande before and after your ride.
